reasoning: The kernel performs 2 global loads and 1 global store (12 bytes transferred) per element, with only 2 FLOPs (one multiply, one add via FMA) per element, yielding an arithmetic intensity of ~0.17 FLOP/byte — far below the A10G ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte. This places the kernel squarely in the memory-bound regime. With 64M elements and a grid-stride loop over contiguous indices, the access pattern achieves good coalescing, so DRAM utilization will be high and bandwidth will be the limiting factor rather than latency or compute.

This places the kernel squarely in the memory-bound regime. With 64M elements and a grid-stride loop over contiguous indices, the access pattern achieves good coalescing, so DRAM utilization will be high and bandwidth will be the limiting factor rather than latency or compute.
